{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_6bc34c25f52344f289194cdb","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t\"\"\"\n2\tSSE broadcaster: stream results to connected surfaces.\n3\t\n4\tManages SSE connections and broadcasts events to relevant surfaces.\n5\t\"\"\"\n6\t\n7\timport asyncio\n8\timport json\n9\timport logging\n10\timport os\n11\tfrom dataclasses import dataclass, field\n12\tfrom datetime import datetime\n13\tfrom typing import Dict\n14\tfrom uuid import uuid4\n15\t\n16\t# Idle-stream keepalive. An SSE connection that sends nothing while it waits for\n17\t# events is indistinguishable from a dead one: the browser's EventSource only\n18\t# fires ``onerror`` when there is I/O to fail, so a *silent* network drop on an\n19\t# idle stream leaves the surface showing \"Connected\" indefinitely (until the\n20\t# OS-level TCP keepalive times out, many minutes later). Emitting a comment\n21\t# line (``\": ping\\n\\n\"``) every few seconds keeps traffic flowing, so:\n22\t#\n23\t#   - proxies/load balancers won't reap the connection as idle, and\n24\t#   - the next ping after a real network drop fails to arrive, the client read\n25\t#     errors, ``onerror`` fires, and the native EventSource reconnects — which\n26\t#     is exactly the resync path the canvas relies on.\n27\t#\n28\t# Comment lines are ignored by EventSource (they never surface as messages), so\n29\t# this adds no client-visible events. ``5s`` is frequent enough that a drop is\n30\t# surfaced well inside any realistic reconnect budget, and quiet enough not to\n31\t# be chatty. Lower it (e.g. via ADC_SSE_KEEPALIVE_SECONDS) for faster\n32\t# reconnection tests.\n33\tKEEPALIVE_INTERVAL_SECONDS = float(\n34\t    os.environ.get(\"ADC_SSE_KEEPALIVE_SECONDS\", \"5\")\n35\t)\n36\t\n37\t# Sentinel pushed onto a connection's queue by :meth:`SSEBroadcaster.drop_session`\n38\t# to make that connection's :meth:`event_generator` return ABRUPTLY — i.e. without\n39\t# emitting a ``disconnect`` event. An abrupt end of the response body is what the\n40\t# browser's ``EventSource`` treats as a dropped connection: it fires ``onerror``\n41\t# and performs its NATIVE auto-reconnect. (The graceful ``disconnect`` event, by\n42\t# contrast, makes the client call ``close()`` and stay down.) This is the only\n43\t# way to faithfully simulate a real proxy/server connection drop from a test\n44\t# against a loopback server — Playwright ``context.set_offline`` cannot break an\n45\t# already-established loopback SSE stream (see tests/e2e/_probe_offline.py).\n46\t_DROP = object()\n47\t\n48\t\n49\tlogger = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n50\t\n51\t\n52\t@dataclass\n53\tclass SSEConnection:\n54\t    \"\"\"An active SSE connection.\"\"\"\n55\t    connection_id: str\n56\t    surface_id: str\n57\t    session_id: str\n58\t    surface_type: str\n59\t    queue: asyncio.Queue = field(default_factory=asyncio.Queue)\n60\t    connected_at: float = field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now().timestamp())\n61\t    last_heartbeat: float = field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now().timestamp())\n62\t\n63\t\n64\t@dataclass\n65\tclass SSEEvent:\n66\t    \"\"\"An SSE event to broadcast.\"\"\"\n67\t    event_type: str\n68\t    data: dict\n69\t    target_session_id: str | None = None\n70\t    target_surface_id: str | None = None\n71\t    exclude_surface_id: str | None = None\n72\t\n73\t\n74\tclass SSEBroadcaster:\n75\t    \"\"\"\n76\t    Manages SSE connections and broadcasts events.\n77\t\n78\t    Events are queued per-connection and sent via SSE.\n79\t    \"\"\"\n80\t\n81\t    def __init__(self):\n82\t        self.connections: Dict[str, SSEConnection] = {}\n83\t        self._cleanup_task: asyncio.Task | None = None\n84\t        self._running = False\n85\t\n86\t    async def start(self) -> None:\n87\t        \"\"\"Start the broadcaster and cleanup task.\"\"\"\n88\t        self._running = True\n89\t        self._cleanup_task = asyncio.create_task(self._cleanup_loop())\n90\t\n91\t    async def stop(self) -> None:\n92\t        \"\"\"Stop the broadcaster and cleanup task.\"\"\"\n93\t        self._running = False\n94\t        if self._cleanup_task:\n95\t            self._cleanup_task.cancel()\n96\t            try:\n97\t                await self._cleanup_task\n98\t            except asyncio.CancelledError:\n99\t                pass\n100\t\n101\t    def register(\n102\t        self,\n103\t        surface_id: str,\n104\t        session_id: str,\n105\t        surface_type: str,\n106\t    ) -> SSEConnection:\n107\t        \"\"\"Register a new SSE connection.\"\"\"\n108\t        connection_id = str(uuid4())\n109\t        connection = SSEConnection(\n110\t            connection_id=connection_id,\n111\t            surface_id=surface_id,\n112\t            session_id=session_id,\n113\t            surface_type=surface_type,\n114\t        )\n115\t        self.connections[connection_id] = connection\n116\t        logger.info(f\"Registered SSE connection {connection_id} for surface {surface_id}\")\n117\t        return connection\n118\t\n119\t    def unregister(self, connection_id: str) -> None:\n120\t        \"\"\"Unregister an SSE connection.\"\"\"\n121\t        if connection_id in self.connections:\n122\t            conn = self.connections[connection_id]\n123\t            logger.info(f\"Unregistered SSE connection {connection_id} for surface {conn.surface_id}\")\n124\t            del self.connections[connection_id]\n125\t\n126\t    def drop_session(self, session_id: str) -> int:\n127\t        \"\"\"Abruptly drop every live SSE stream for ``session_id``.\n128\t\n129\t        Pushes the ``_DROP`` sentinel onto each matching connection's queue so\n130\t        its :meth:`event_generator` returns without emitting a ``disconnect``\n131\t        event — the browser's ``EventSource`` then sees the stream end abruptly,\n132\t        fires ``onerror``, and performs its native auto-reconnect. Returns the\n133\t        number of streams signalled. Used by ``POST /api/v1/test/drop-sse`` to\n134\t        faithfully simulate a real proxy/server connection drop, which\n135\t        ``context.set_offline`` cannot reproduce against a loopback server\n136\t        (loopback connections are exempt from offline emulation — see\n137\t        tests/e2e/_probe_offline.py).\n138\t        \"\"\"\n139\t        count = 0\n140\t        for conn in self.connections.values():\n141\t            if conn.session_id == session_id:\n142\t                conn.queue.put_nowait(_DROP)\n143\t                count += 1\n144\t        if count:\n145\t            logger.info(f\"Drop requested for session {session_id}: {count} stream(s)\")\n146\t        return count\n147\t\n148\t    def heartbeat(self, connection_id: str) -> bool:\n149\t        \"\"\"Update heartbeat for a connection. Returns True if connection exists.\"\"\"\n150\t        if connection_id in self.connections:\n151\t            self.connections[connection_id].last_heartbeat = datetime.now().timestamp()\n152\t            return True\n153\t        return False\n154\t\n155\t    async def broadcast(self, event: SSEEvent) -> int:\n156\t        \"\"\"\n157\t        Broadcast an event to relevant connections.\n158\t\n159\t        Returns the number of connections the event was sent to.\n160\t        \"\"\"\n161\t        sent_count = 0\n162\t\n163\t        for conn in list(self.connections.values()):\n164\t            # Filter by target\n165\t            if event.target_session_id and conn.session_id != event.target_session_id:\n166\t                continue\n167\t            if event.target_surface_id and conn.surface_id != event.target_surface_id:\n168\t                continue\n169\t            if event.exclude_surface_id and conn.surface_id == event.exclude_surface_id:\n170\t                continue\n171\t\n172\t            # Queue the event\n173\t            try:\n174\t                conn.queue.put_nowait(event)\n175\t                sent_count += 1\n176\t            except asyncio.QueueFull:\n177\t                logger.warning(f\"Queue full for connection {conn.connection_id}, dropping event\")\n178\t\n179\t        return sent_count\n180\t\n181\t    async def event_generator(self, connection: SSEConnection):\n182\t        \"\"\"\n183\t        Generate SSE events for a connection.\n184\t\n185\t        Yields formatted SSE messages.\n186\t        \"\"\"\n187\t        try:\n188\t            # Send initial connection event\n189\t            yield self._format_sse(\"connected\", {\n190\t                \"connection_id\": connection.connection_id,\n191\t                \"surface_id\": connection.surface_id,\n192\t                \"session_id\": connection.session_id,\n193\t            })\n194\t\n195\t            while True:\n196\t                # Block on the next event, but no longer than the keepalive\n197\t                # interval: if nothing arrives, emit a comment-line ping so the\n198\t                # stream is never silent. A silent idle stream is indistinguish-\n199\t                # able from a dead one to the browser — EventSource only fires\n200\t                # ``onerror`` when there is I/O to fail — so without these pings\n201\t                # a real network drop on an idle surface leaves it showing\n202\t                # \"Connected\" until the OS TCP keepalive times out (minutes\n203\t                # later). The ping is a comment line (``\": ping\\n\\n\"``), which\n204\t                # EventSource ignores — it surfaces no message to the client.\n205\t                try:\n206\t                    event = await asyncio.wait_for(\n207\t                        connection.queue.get(),\n208\t                        timeout=KEEPALIVE_INTERVAL_SECONDS,\n209\t                    )\n210\t                except asyncio.TimeoutError:\n211\t                    yield \": ping\\n\\n\"\n212\t                    continue\n213\t\n214\t                # Abrupt drop sentinel (see _DROP): end the stream WITHOUT a\n215\t                # disconnect event so the browser EventSource onerrors + reconnects.\n216\t                if event is _DROP:\n217\t                    logger.info(f\"Drop sentinel on connection {connection.connection_id}\")\n218\t                    return\n219\t\n220\t                connection.last_heartbeat = datetime.now().timestamp()\n221\t\n222\t                # Format and yield the event\n223\t                yield self._format_sse(event.event_type, event.data)\n224\t\n225\t                # Special case: disconnect event ends the stream\n226\t                if event.event_type == \"disconnect\":\n227\t                    logger.info(f\"Disconnect event for connection {connection.connection_id}\")\n228\t                    break\n229\t\n230\t        except asyncio.CancelledError:\n231\t            logger.info(f\"Connection {connection.connection_id} cancelled\")\n232\t            raise\n233\t        finally:\n234\t            self.unregister(connection.connection_id)\n235\t\n236\t    def _format_sse(self, event_type: str, data: dict) -> str:\n237\t        \"\"\"Format an event as SSE.\"\"\"\n238\t        return f\"event: {event_type}\\ndata: {json.dumps(data)}\\n\\n\"\n239\t\n240\t    async def _cleanup_loop(self):\n241\t        \"\"\"Periodically clean up dead connections.\"\"\"\n242\t        while self._running:\n243\t            try:\n244\t                await asyncio.sleep(30)  # Check every 30 seconds\n245\t\n246\t                now = datetime.now().timestamp()\n247\t                timeout = 300  # 5 minutes\n248\t\n249\t                dead_connections = [\n250\t                    cid for cid, conn in self.connections.items()\n251\t                    if (now - conn.last_heartbeat) > timeout\n252\t                ]\n253\t\n254\t                for cid in dead_connections:\n255\t                    logger.info(f\"Cleaning up dead connection {cid}\")\n256\t                    # Send disconnect event before removing\n257\t                    try:\n258\t                        conn = self.connections[cid]\n259\t                        conn.queue.put_nowait(SSEEvent(\n260\t                            event_type=\"disconnect\",\n261\t                            data={\"reason\": \"timeout\"},\n262\t                        ))\n263\t                    except asyncio.QueueFull:\n264\t                        pass\n265\t                    self.unregister(cid)\n266\t\n267\t            except asyncio.CancelledError:\n268\t                break\n269\t            except Exception as e:\n270\t                logger.error(f\"Error in cleanup loop: {e}\")\n271\t\n272\t\n273\t# Global broadcaster instance\n274\t_broadcaster: SSEBroadcaster | None = None\n275\t\n276\t\n277\tdef get_broadcaster() -> SSEBroadcaster:\n278\t    \"\"\"Get or create the global SSE broadcaster.\"\"\"\n279\t    global _broadcaster\n280\t    if _broadcaster is None:\n281\t        _broadcaster = SSEBroadcaster()\n282\t    return _broadcaster\n283\t\n284\t\n285\t# Event types\n286\tclass EventType:\n287\t    \"\"\"SSE event types.\"\"\"\n288\t\n289\t    # Connection lifecycle\n290\t    CONNECTED = \"connected\"\n291\t    DISCONNECT = \"disconnect\"\n292\t    HEARTBEAT = \"heartbeat\"\n293\t\n294\t    # Result events\n295\t    RESULT_CREATED = \"result_created\"\n296\t    RESULT_UPDATED = \"result_updated\"\n297\t\n298\t    # Component events (Phase 2)\n299\t    COMPONENT_UPDATED = \"component_updated\"\n300\t\n301\t    # Intent events\n302\t    INTENT_PENDING = \"intent_pending\"\n303\t    INTENT_DISPATCHED = \"intent_dispatched\"\n304\t    INTENT_RESOLVED = \"intent_resolved\"\n305\t\n306\t    # Topic events\n307\t    TOPIC_CREATED = \"topic_created\"\n308\t    TOPIC_UPDATED = \"topic_updated\"\n309\t    TOPIC_STALE = \"topic_stale\"\n310\t\n311\t    # broadcaster: stream results to connected surfaces.\n\nManages SSE connections and broadcasts events to relevant surfaces.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport asyncio\nimport json\nimport logging\nimport os\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass, field\nfrom datetime import datetime\nfrom typing import Dict\nfrom uuid import uuid4\n\n# Idle-stream keepalive. An SSE connection that sends nothing while it waits for\n# events is indistinguishable from a dead one: the browser's EventSource only\n# fires ``onerror`` when there is I/O to fail, so a *silent* network drop on an\n# idle stream leaves the surface showing \"Connected\" indefinitely (until the\n# OS-level TCP keepalive times out, many minutes later). Emitting a comment\n# line (``\": ping\\n\\n\"``) every few seconds keeps traffic flowing, so:\n#\n#   - proxies/load balancers won't reap the connection as idle, and\n#   - the next ping after a real network drop fails to arrive, the client read\n#     errors, ``onerror`` fires, and the native EventSource reconnects — which\n#     is exactly the resync path the canvas relies on.\n#\n# Comment lines are ignored by EventSource (they never surface as messages), so\n# this adds no client-visible events. ``5s`` is frequent enough that a drop is\n# surfaced well inside any realistic reconnect budget, and quiet enough not to\n# be chatty. Lower it (e.g. via ADC_SSE_KEEPALIVE_SECONDS) for faster\n# reconnection tests.\nKEEPALIVE_INTERVAL_SECONDS = float(\n    os.environ.get(\"ADC_SSE_KEEPALIVE_SECONDS\", \"5\")\n)\n\n# Sentinel pushed onto a connection's queue by :meth:`SSEBroadcaster.drop_session`\n# to make that connection's :meth:`event_generator` return ABRUPTLY — i.e. without\n# emitting a ``disconnect`` event. An abrupt end of the response body is what the\n# browser's ``EventSource`` treats as a dropped connection: it fires ``onerror``\n# and performs its NATIVE auto-reconnect. (The graceful ``disconnect`` event, by\n# contrast, makes the client call ``close()`` and stay down.) This is the only\n# way to faithfully simulate a real proxy/server connection drop from a test\n# against a loopback server — Playwright ``context.set_offline`` cannot break an\n# already-established loopback SSE stream (see tests/e2e/_probe_offline.py).\n_DROP = object()\n\n\nlogger = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass SSEConnection:\n    \"\"\"An active SSE connection.\"\"\"\n    connection_id: str\n    surface_id: str\n    session_id: str\n    surface_type: str\n    queue: asyncio.Queue = field(default_factory=asyncio.Queue)\n    connected_at: float = field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now().timestamp())\n    last_heartbeat: float = field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now().timestamp())\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass SSEEvent:\n    \"\"\"An SSE event to broadcast.\"\"\"\n    event_type: str\n    data: dict\n    target_session_id: str | None = None\n    target_surface_id: str | None = None\n    exclude_surface_id: str | None = None\n\n\nclass SSEBroadcaster:\n    \"\"\"\n    Manages SSE connections and broadcasts events.\n\n    Events are queued per-connection and sent via SSE.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self):\n        self.connections: Dict[str, SSEConnection] = {}\n        self._cleanup_task: asyncio.Task | None = None\n        self._running = False\n\n    async def start(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Start the broadcaster and cleanup task.\"\"\"\n        self._running = True\n        self._cleanup_task = asyncio.create_task(self._cleanup_loop())\n\n    async def stop(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Stop the broadcaster and cleanup task.\"\"\"\n        self._running = False\n        if self._cleanup_task:\n            self._cleanup_task.cancel()\n            try:\n                await self._cleanup_task\n            except asyncio.CancelledError:\n                pass\n\n    def register(\n        self,\n        surface_id: str,\n        session_id: str,\n        surface_type: str,\n    ) -> SSEConnection:\n        \"\"\"Register a new SSE connection.\"\"\"\n        connection_id = str(uuid4())\n        connection = SSEConnection(\n            connection_id=connection_id,\n            surface_id=surface_id,\n            session_id=session_id,\n            surface_type=surface_type,\n        )\n        self.connections[connection_id] = connection\n        logger.info(f\"Registered SSE connection {connection_id} for surface {surface_id}\")\n        return connection\n\n    def unregister(self, connection_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Unregister an SSE connection.\"\"\"\n        if connection_id in self.connections:\n            conn = self.connections[connection_id]\n            logger.info(f\"Unregistered SSE connection {connection_id} for surface {conn.surface_id}\")\n            del self.connections[connection_id]\n\n    def drop_session(self, session_id: str) -> int:\n        \"\"\"Abruptly drop every live SSE stream for ``session_id``.\n\n        Pushes the ``_DROP`` sentinel onto each matching connection's queue so\n        its :meth:`event_generator` returns without emitting a ``disconnect``\n        event — the browser's ``EventSource`` then sees the stream end abruptly,\n        fires ``onerror``, and performs its native auto-reconnect. Returns the\n        number of streams signalled. Used by ``POST /api/v1/test/drop-sse`` to\n        faithfully simulate a real proxy/server connection drop, which\n        ``context.set_offline`` cannot reproduce against a loopback server\n        (loopback connections are exempt from offline emulation — see\n        tests/e2e/_probe_offline.py).\n        \"\"\"\n        count = 0\n        for conn in self.connections.values():\n            if conn.session_id == session_id:\n                conn.queue.put_nowait(_DROP)\n                count += 1\n        if count:\n            logger.info(f\"Drop requested for session {session_id}: {count} stream(s)\")\n        return count\n\n    def heartbeat(self, connection_id: str) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Update heartbeat for a connection. Returns True if connection exists.\"\"\"\n        if connection_id in self.connections:\n            self.connections[connection_id].last_heartbeat = datetime.now().timestamp()\n            return True\n        return False\n\n    async def broadcast(self, event: SSEEvent) -> int:\n        \"\"\"\n        Broadcast an event to relevant connections.\n\n        Returns the number of connections the event was sent to.\n        \"\"\"\n        sent_count = 0\n\n        for conn in list(self.connections.values()):\n            # Filter by target\n            if event.target_session_id and conn.session_id != event.target_session_id:\n                continue\n            if event.target_surface_id and conn.surface_id != event.target_surface_id:\n                continue\n            if event.exclude_surface_id and conn.surface_id == event.exclude_surface_id:\n                continue\n\n            # Queue the event\n            try:\n                conn.queue.put_nowait(event)\n                sent_count += 1\n            except asyncio.QueueFull:\n                logger.warning(f\"Queue full for connection {conn.connection_id}, dropping event\")\n\n        return sent_count\n\n    async def event_generator(self, connection: SSEConnection):\n        \"\"\"\n        Generate SSE events for a connection.\n\n        Yields formatted SSE messages.\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            # Send initial connection event\n            yield self._format_sse(\"connected\", {\n                \"connection_id\": connection.connection_id,\n                \"surface_id\": connection.surface_id,\n                \"session_id\": connection.session_id,\n            })\n\n            while True:\n                # Block on the next event, but no longer than the keepalive\n                # interval: if nothing arrives, emit a comment-line ping so the\n                # stream is never silent. A silent idle stream is indistinguish-\n                # able from a dead one to the browser — EventSource only fires\n                # ``onerror`` when there is I/O to fail — so without these pings\n                # a real network drop on an idle surface leaves it showing\n                # \"Connected\" until the OS TCP keepalive times out (minutes\n                # later). The ping is a comment line (``\": ping\\n\\n\"``), which\n                # EventSource ignores — it surfaces no message to the client.\n                try:\n                    event = await asyncio.wait_for(\n                        connection.queue.get(),\n                        timeout=KEEPALIVE_INTERVAL_SECONDS,\n                    )\n                except asyncio.TimeoutError:\n                    yield \": ping\\n\\n\"\n                    continue\n\n                # Abrupt drop sentinel (see _DROP): end the stream WITHOUT a\n                # disconnect event so the browser EventSource onerrors + reconnects.\n                if event is _DROP:\n                    logger.info(f\"Drop sentinel on connection {connection.connection_id}\")\n                    return\n\n                connection.last_heartbeat = datetime.now().timestamp()\n\n                # Format and yield the event\n                yield self._format_sse(event.event_type, event.data)\n\n                # Special case: disconnect event ends the stream\n                if event.event_type == \"disconnect\":\n                    logger.info(f\"Disconnect event for connection {connection.connection_id}\")\n                    break\n\n        except asyncio.CancelledError:\n            logger.info(f\"Connection {connection.connection_id} cancelled\")\n            raise\n        finally:\n            self.unregister(connection.connection_id)\n\n    def _format_sse(self, event_type: str, data: dict) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Format an event as SSE.\"\"\"\n        return f\"event: {event_type}\\ndata: {json.dumps(data)}\\n\\n\"\n\n    async def _cleanup_loop(self):\n        \"\"\"Periodically clean up dead connections.\"\"\"\n        while self._running:\n            try:\n                await asyncio.sleep(30)  # Check every 30 seconds\n\n                now = datetime.now().timestamp()\n                timeout = 300  # 5 minutes\n\n                dead_connections = [\n                    cid for cid, conn in self.connections.items()\n                    if (now - conn.last_heartbeat) > timeout\n                ]\n\n                for cid in dead_connections:\n                    logger.info(f\"Cleaning up dead connection {cid}\")\n                    # Send disconnect event before removing\n                    try:\n                        conn = self.connections[cid]\n                        conn.queue.put_nowait(SSEEvent(\n                            event_type=\"disconnect\",\n                            data={\"reason\": \"timeout\"},\n                        ))\n                    except asyncio.QueueFull:\n                        pass\n                    self.unregister(cid)\n\n            except asyncio.CancelledError:\n                break\n            except Exception as e:\n                logger.error(f\"Error in cleanup loop: {e}\")\n\n\n# Global broadcaster instance\n_broadcaster: SSEBroadcaster | None = None\n\n\ndef get_broadcaster() -> SSEBroadcaster:\n    \"\"\"Get or create the global SSE broadcaster.\"\"\"\n    global _broadcaster\n    if _broadcaster is None:\n        _broadcaster = SSEBroadcaster()\n    return _broadcaster\n\n\n# Event types\nclass EventType:\n    \"\"\"SSE event types.\"\"\"\n\n    # Connection lifecycle\n    CONNECTED = \"connected\"\n    DISCONNECT = \"disconnect\"\n    HEARTBEAT = \"heartbeat\"\n\n    # Result events\n    RESULT_CREATED = \"result_created\"\n    RESULT_UPDATED = \"result_updated\"\n\n    # Component events (Phase 2)\n    COMPONENT_UPDATED = \"component_updated\"\n\n    # Intent events\n    INTENT_PENDING = \"intent_pending\"\n    INTENT_DISPATCHED = \"intent_dispatched\"\n    INTENT_RESOLVED = \"intent_resolved\"\n\n    # Topic events\n    TOPIC_CREATED = \"topic_created\"\n    TOPIC_UPDATED = \"topic_updated\"\n    TOPIC_STALE = \"topic_stale\"\n\n    # Workload events\n    WORKLOAD_SUMMARY = \"workload_summary\"\n    EXCEPTION_RAISED = \"exception_raised\"\n\n    # Bead events\n    BEAD_CLOSED = \"bead_closed\"\n    BEAD_FAILED = \"bead_failed\"\n\n    # Circuit breaker events\n    TASK_STUCK = \"task_stuck\"\n    TASK_FAILED = \"task_failed\"\n\n\nasync def broadcast_result(\n    result: dict,\n    session_id: str,\n    target_surface_id: str | None = None,\n) -> int:\n    \"\"\"\n    Broadcast a result to relevant surfaces.\n\n    Returns the number of connections the event was sent to.\n    \"\"\"\n    broadcaster = get_broadcaster()\n    event = SSEEvent(\n        event_type=EventType.RESULT_CREATED,\n        data=result,\n        target_session_id=session_id,\n        target_surface_id=target_surface_id,\n    )\n    return await broadcaster.broadcast(event)\n\n\nasync def broadcast_intent_update(\n    intent: dict,\n    session_id: str,\n    event_type: str = EventType.INTENT_RESOLVED,\n) -> int:\n    \"\"\"Broadcast an intent status update.\"\"\"\n    broadcaster = get_broadcaster()\n    event = SSEEvent(\n        event_type=event_type,\n        data=intent,\n        target_session_id=session_id,\n    )\n    return await broadcaster.broadcast(event)\n\n\nasync def broadcast_workload_summary(\n    session_id: str,\n    summary: dict,\n    surface_id: str | None = None,\n) -> int:\n    \"\"\"Broadcast a workload summary (for reconnection).\"\"\"\n    broadcaster = get_broadcaster()\n    event = SSEEvent(\n        event_type=EventType.WORKLOAD_SUMMARY,\n        data=summary,\n        target_session_id=session_id,\n        target_surface_id=surface_id,\n    )\n    return await broadcaster.broadcast(event)\n","numLines":373,"startLine":1,"totalLines":373}}}

Extract context for rule evaluation\n171\t        context = {\n172\t            \"environment\": request.metadata.get(\"environment\"),\n173\t            \"project_slug\": request.project_slug,\n174\t            \"branch\": request.metadata.get(\"branch\"),\n175\t            \"action\": request.metadata.get(\"action\"),\n176\t            \"risk_level\": request.metadata.get(\"risk_level\", \"normal\"),\n177\t        }\n178\t\n179\t        # Check safe mutations with conditions\n186\t        safe_mutations = auto_approve_rules.get(\"safe_mutations\", [])\n187\t        for rule in safe_mutations:\n188\t            condition = rule.get(\"condition\", \"\")\n189\t            actions = rule.get(\"actions\", [])\n190\t\n191\t            # Simple condition evaluation (supports basic comparisons)\n192\t            if self._evaluate_condition(condition, context) and action in actions:\n193\t                return True, f\"Safe mutation in {condition}: {action}\"\n194\t\n195\t        # Check manual approval rules (never auto-approve)\n196\t        manual_rules = exceptions_config.get(\"manual_approval\", [])\n197\t        for rule in manual_rules:\n198\t            condition = rule.get(\"condition\", \"\")\n199\t            actions = rule.get(\"actions\", [])\n200\t\n201\t            # Check if the action is in this rule's actions\n202\t            if action not in actions:\n203\t                continue\n204\t\n205\t            # Evaluate the condition\n206\t            if self._evaluate_condition(condition, context):\n207\t                if rule.get(\"always_approve\") is False:\n208\t                    return False, f\"Manual approval required: {condition}\"\n209\t\n210\t        # Check approval workflow rules\n211\t        approval_config = exceptions_config.get(\"approval\", {})\n212\t        never_auto_approve = approval_config.get(\"never_auto_approve\", [])\n213\t        for condition_str in never_auto_approve:\n214\t            if self._evaluate_condition(condition_str, context):\n215\t                return False, f\"Never auto-approve: {condition_str}\"\n216\t\n217\t        # Default: require manual approval for unknown actions\n218\t        return False, \"Unknown action - requires manual approval\"\n219\t\n220\t    def _evaluate_condition(self, condition: str, context: dict) -> bool:\n221\t        \"\"\"\n222\t        Evaluate a condition string against context.\n223\t\n224\t        Supports simple conditions like:\n225\t        - \"environment == 'staging'\"\n226\t        - \"branch == 'main' || branch == 'master'\"  (using || for OR)\n227\t        - \"action == 'kubectl_delete_namespace'\"\n228\t        - \"environment == 'staging' && action == 'restart'\"  (using && for AND)\n229\t\n230\t        Note: Supports both shell-style operators (||, &&) and Python-style (or, and).\n231\t\n232\t        Args:\n233\t            condition: The condition string\n234\t            context: Dict of available variables\n235\t\n236\t        Returns:\n237\t            True if condition evaluates to True, False otherwise\n238\t        \"\"\"\n239\t        try:\n240\t            # Translate shell-style operators to Python syntax\n241\t            # (People are more familiar with || and && from bash/js)\n242\t            python_condition = condition.replace(\" || \", \" or \").replace(\" && \", \" and \")\n243\t\n244\t            # Create a safe evaluation environment\n245\t            safe_globals = {\"__builtins__\": {}}\n246\t            safe_locals = {\n247\t                \"environment\": context.get(\"environment\"),\n248\t                \"project_slug\": context.get(\"project_slug\"),\n249\t                \"branch\": context.get(\"branch\"),\n250\t                \"action\": context.get(\"action\"),\n251\t                \"risk_level\": context.get(\"risk_level\"),\n252\t            }\n253\t\n254\t            # Evaluate condition\n255\t            result = eval(python_condition, safe_globals, safe_locals)\n256\t            return bool(result)\n257\t        except Exception as e:\n258\t            logger.warning(f\"Failed to evaluate condition '{condition}': {e}\")\n259\t            return False\n260\t\n261\t    def _get_bead_type_from_targets(self, intent_type: str, exceptions_config: dict) -> str:\n262\t        \"\"\"\n263\t        Get the bead type from escalation_targets config.\n264\t\n265\t        Args:\n266\t            intent_type: The intent type from IntentRouter (e.g., 'action', 'task-profile', 'self-modification')\n267\t            exceptions_config: The loaded exceptions.yaml config\n268\t\n269\t        Returns:\n270\t            The bead type to create (default: 'task')\n271\t        \"\"\"\n272\t        # Map IntentRouter types to escalation_targets keys\n273\t        intent_type_mapping = {\n274\t            \"action\": \"action\",\n275\t            \"task-profile\": \"action\",  # Task-profile intents create action-type beads\n276\t            \"self-modification\": \"self_modification\",\n277\t            \"monitoring-config\": \"monitoring_config\",\n278\t        }\n279\t\n280\t        # Map the intent type to escalation_targets key\n281\t        escalation_key = intent_type_mapping.get(intent_type, intent_type)\n282\t\n283\t        escalation_targets = exceptions_config.get(\"escalation_targets\", {})\n284\t        target_config = escalation_targets.get(escalation_key, {})\n285\t        return target_config.get(\"bead_type\", \"task\")\n286\t\n287\t    async def _execute_auto_approved(self, request: EscalateRequest) -> dict:\n288\t        \"\"\"\n289\t        Execute an auto-approved action directly.\n290\t\n291\t        Executes kubectl and git commands that are auto-approved based on\n292\t        exceptions.yaml rules, without creating beads.\n293\t\n294\t        Args:\n295\t            request: The escalate request\n296\t\n297\t        Returns:\n298\t            Execution result dict\n299\t        \"\"\"\n300\t        action = request.metadata.get(\"action\", \"\")\n301\t        logger.info(f\"Executing auto-approved action '{action}' for intent {request.intent_id}\")\n302\t\n303\t        try:\n304\t            # Route to appropriate executor based on action type\n305\t            if action == \"kubectl_delete_pod\":\n306\t                return await self._execute_delete_pod(request)\n307\t            elif action.startswith(\"kubectl_\"):\n308\t                return await self._execute_kubectl_command(request)\n309\t            elif action.startswith(\"git_\"):\n310\t                return await self._execute_git_command(request)\n311\t            else:\n312\t                # Unknown action - return placeholder for now\n313\t                logger.warning(f\"Unknown auto-approved action: {action}\")\n314\t                return {\n315\t                    \"status\": \"pending\",\n316\t                    \"summary\": f\"Auto-approved action '{action}' not yet implemented\",\n317\t                    \"data\": {\n318\t                        \"action\": action,\n319\t                        \"utterance\": request.utterance,\n320\t                    },\n321\t                    \"urgency\": \"low\",\n322\t                }\n323\t\n324\t        except CommandExecutionError as e:\n325\t            logger.error(f\"Command execution failed for intent {request.intent_id}: {e}\")\n326\t            return {\n327\t                \"status\": \"failed\",\n328\t                \"summary\": f\"Command execution failed: {str(e)}\",\n329\t                \"data\": {\n330\t                    \"action\": action,\n331\t                    \"error\": str(e),\n332\t                },\n333\t                \"urgency\": \"normal\",\n334\t            }\n335\t        except Exception as e:\n336\t            logger.error(f\"Unexpected error executing auto-approved action: {e}\")\n337\t            return {\n338\t                \"status\": \"failed\",\n339\t                \"summary\": f\"Unexpected error: {str(e)}\",\n340\t                \"data\": {\n341\t                    \"action\": action,\n342\t                    \"error\": str(e),\n343\t                },\n344\t                \"urgency\": \"normal\",\n345\t            }\n346\t\n347\t    async def _execute_delete_pod(self, request: EscalateRequest) -> dict:\n348\t        \"\"\"\n349\t        Execute kubectl delete pod command.\n350\t\n351\t        Args:\n352\t            request: The escalate request\n353\t\n354\t        Returns:\n355\t            Execution result dict\n356\t        \"\"\"\n357\t        executor = get_kubectl_executor()\n358\t\n359\t        # TODO: Implement other kubectl commands\n385\t        action = request.metadata.get(\"action\", \"\")\n386\t        return {\n387\t            \"status\": \"pending\",\n388\t            \"summary\": f\"Kubectl command '{action}' not yet implemented\",\n389\t            \"data\": {\n390\t                \"action\": action,\n391\t                \"utterance\": request.utterance,\n392\t            },\n393\t            \"urgency\": \"low\",\n394\t        }\n395\t\n396\t    async def _execute_git_command(self, request: EscalateRequest) -> dict:\n397\t        \"\"\"\n398\t        Execute git commands (placeholder for future).\n399\t\n400\t        Args:\n401\t            request: The escalate request\n402\t\n403\t        Returns:\n404\t            Execution result dict\n405\t        \"\"\"\n406\t        # Build context for the LLM\n431\t        context_parts = [\n432\t            f\"User utterance: {request.utterance}\",\n433\t            f\"Intent type: {request.intent_type}\",\n434\t        ]\n435\t\n436\t        if request.project_slug:\n437\t            context_parts.append(f\"Project: {request.project_slug}\")\n438\t\n439\t        if request.topic_id:\n440\t            context_parts.append(f\"Topic ID: {request.topic_id}\")\n441\t\n442\t        # Add any additional context\n443\t        if request.context:\n444\t            context_parts.append(f\"\\nAdditional context:\")\n445\t            for key, value in request.context.items():\n446\t                context_parts.append(f\"  {key}: {value}\")\n447\t\n448\t        # Build the user message\n449\t        user_message = \"\\n\".join([\n450\t            \"Formulate a NEEDLE bead body for this intent:\",\n451\t            \"\",\n452\t            *context_parts,\n453\t            \"\",\n454\t            \"The bead body should be clear, actionable, and structured for Claude Code execution.\",\n455\t        ])\n456\t\n457\t        logger.info(f\"Formulating bead body for intent {request.intent_id}\")\n458\t\n459\t        # Make LLM call\n460\t        try:\n461\t            bead_body = await client.call_simple(\n462\t                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n463\t                user_message=user_message,\n464\t                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,\n465\t                max_tokens=4096,\n466\t                temperature=0.7,\n467\t            )\n468\t\n469\t            logger.info(f\"Formulated bead body for intent {request.intent_id}: {len(bead_body)} chars\")\n470\t            return bead_body\n471\t\n472\t        except Exception as e:\n473\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to formulate bead body: {e}\")\n474\t            raise EscalateError(f\"Failed to formulate bead body: {e}\") from e\n475\t\n476\t    async def create_bead(\n477\t        self,\n478\t        request: EscalateRequest,\n479\t        bead_body: str,\n480\t    ) -> str:\n481\t        \"\"\"\n482\t        Create a bead using the bf CLI.\n483\t\n484\t        Returns the bead ID.\n485\t        \"\"\"\n486\t        # Generate bead title from intent\n487\t        title = self._generate_bead_title(request)\n488\t\n489\t        # Prepare metadata (includes session_id for bead watcher routing)\n490\t        metadata = {\n491\t            \"session_id\": request.session_id,\n492\t            \"intent_id\": request.intent_id,\n493\t            \"intent_type\": request.intent_type,\n494\t            \"origin_surface_id\": request.metadata.get(\"surface_id\"),\n495\t            \"created_at\": int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n496\t        }\n497\t\n498\t        if request.project_slug:\n499\t            metadata[\"project_slug\"] = request.project_slug\n500\t\n501\t        if request.topic_id:\n502\t            metadata[\"topic_id\"] = request.topic_id\n503\t\n504\t        # Build bf create command\n505\t        # Using bf CLI to create the bead\n506\t        # Note: bf uses --description for the main body content\n507\t        try:\n508\t            # Build command with description (escape bead body for shell)\n509\t            cmd = [\n510\t                \"bf\",\n511\t                \"create\",\n512\t                \"--title\", title,\n513\t                \"--type\", \"task\",  # task-profile intents create task beads\n514\t                \"--description\", bead_body,\n515\t            ]\n516\t\n517\t            # Add labels for metadata tracking\n518\t            # Labels are simple strings, we'll encode key=value for structured data\n519\t            for key, value in metadata.items():\n520\t                if value is not None:\n521\t                    cmd.extend([\"--label\", f\"{key}={value}\"])\n522\t\n523\t            proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n524\t                *cmd,\n525\t                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n526\t                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n527\t            )\n528\t\n529\t            stdout, stderr = await proc.communicate()\n530\t\n531\t            if proc.returncode != 0:\n532\t                error_msg = stderr.decode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\")\n533\t                logger.error(f\"bf create failed: {error_msg}\")\n534\t                raise BeadCreationError(f\"bf create failed: {error_msg}\")\n535\t\n536\t            # Parse bead ID from output\n537\t            output = stdout.decode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\")\n538\t            bead_id = self._extract_bead_id(output)\n539\t\n540\t            logger.info(f\"Created bead {bead_id} for intent {request.intent_id}\")\n541\t            return bead_id\n542\t\n543\t        except FileNotFoundError:\n544\t            logger.error(\"bf CLI not found\")\n545\t            raise BeadCreationError(\"bf CLI not found\")\n546\t        except Exception as e:\n547\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to create bead: {e}\")\n548\t            raise BeadCreationError(f\"Failed to create bead: {e}\") from e\n549\t\n550\t    async def _create_bead_with_type(\n551\t        self,\n552\t        request: EscalateRequest,\n553\t        bead_body: str,\n554\t        bead_type: str,\n555\t    ) -> str:\n556\t        \"\"\"\n557\t        Create a bead with a specific type using the bf CLI.\n558\t\n559\t        Returns the bead ID.\n560\t        \"\"\"\n561\t        # Generate bead title from intent\n562\t        title = self._generate_bead_title(request)\n563\t\n564\t        # Prepare metadata (includes session_id for bead watcher routing)\n565\t        metadata = {\n566\t            \"session_id\": request.session_id,\n567\t            \"intent_id\": request.intent_id,\n568\t            \"intent_type\": request.intent_type,\n569\t            \"origin_surface_id\": request.metadata.get(\"surface_id\"),\n570\t            \"created_at\": int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n571\t        }\n572\t\n573\t        if request.project_slug:\n574\t            metadata[\"project_slug\"] = request.project_slug\n575\t\n576\t        if request.topic_id:\n577\t            metadata[\"topic_id\"] = request.topic_id\n578\t\n579\t        # Add labels for metadata tracking\n590\t            for key, value in metadata.items():\n591\t                if value is not None:\n592\t                    cmd.extend([\"--label\", f\"{key}={value}\"])\n593\t\n594\t            proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n595\t                *cmd,\n596\t                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n597\t                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n598\t            )\n599\t\n600\t            stdout, stderr = await proc.communicate()\n601\t\n602\t            if proc.returncode != 0:\n603\t                error_msg = stderr.decode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\")\n604\t                logger.error(f\"bf create failed: {error_msg}\")\n605\t                raise BeadCreationError(f\"bf create failed: {error_msg}\")\n606\t\n607\t            # Check for per-project SLA override\n642\t        if project_slug:\n643\t            project = get_project(project_slug)\n644\t            if project:\n645\t                sla_hours = project.get(\"sla_hours\")\n646\t                if sla_hours is not None:\n647\t                    logger.info(\n648\t                        f\"Using per-project SLA override for {project_slug}: \"\n649\t                        f\"{sla_hours} hours\"\n650\t                    )\n651\t\n652\t        store = await self._get_store()\n653\t        await store.create_bead_watch(\n654\t            bead_ref=bead_ref,\n655\t            sla_hours=sla_hours,\n656\t            intent_type=intent_type,\n657\t        )\n658\t        logger.debug(f\"Created bead_watch row for {bead_ref}\")\n659\t\n660\t    def _generate_bead_title(self, request: EscalateRequest) -> str:\n661\t        \"\"\"Generate a bead title from the request.\"\"\"\n662\t        # Use utterance prefix as title\n663\t        utterance = request.utterance.strip()\n664\t        if len(utterance) > 60:\n665\t            title = utterance[:57] + \"...\"\n666\t        else:\n667\t            title = utterance\n668\t\n669\t        # Add project prefix if available\n670\t        if request.project_slug:\n671\t            title = f\"[{request.project_slug}] {title}\"\n672\t\n673\t        return title\n674\t\n675\t    def _extract_bead_id(self, output: str) -> str:\n676\t        \"\"\"Extract bead ID from bf create output.\n677\t\n678\t        bf create outputs just the bead ID (e.g., \"abc-123\").\n679\t        May include prefix text like \"Created bead abc-123\" in some versions.\n680\t        \"\"\"\n681\t        import re\n682\t\n683\t        # Strip whitespace first\n684\t        output = output.strip()\n685\t\n686\t        if not output:\n687\t            logger.warning(f\"Empty bf create output\")\n688\t            return str(uuid.uuid4())\n689\t\n690\t        # Try to extract bead ID pattern: lowercase letters, dash, alphanumeric\n691\t        # Bead IDs typically include numbers like: abc-123, xyz-789, adc-2fs\n692\t        # We prioritize matches with digits over letter-only matches\n693\t        patterns = [\n694\t            r\"[a-z]+-[a-z]+-[a-z0-9]*[0-9]+[a-z0-9]*\",  # Three components with digit: test-bead-456\n695\t            r\"[a-z]+-[a-z0-9]*[0-9]+[a-z0-9]*\",          # Two components with digit: abc-123\n696\t            r\"[a-z]+-[a-z]+-[a-z0-9]+\",                  # Three components letter-only: test-bead-xyz\n697\t            r\"[a-z]{3,}-[a-z0-9]{2,}\",                   # Fallback: any 3+ char prefix, dash, 2+ char suffix\n698\t        ]\n699\t\n700\t        for pattern in patterns:\n701\t            matches = re.findall(pattern, output)\n702\t            if matches:\n703\t                # Return the longest match (prefer more specific patterns)\n704\t                bead_id = max(matches, key=len)\n705\t                logger.debug(f\"Extracted bead ID '{bead_id}' from output\")\n706\t                return bead_id\n707\t\n708\t        # Fallback: if output already looks like a bead ID, return as-is\n709\t        # Basic validation: should contain a dash and be reasonable length\n710\t        if \"-\" in output and len(output) >= 3:\n711\t            logger.warning(f\"Using output as bead ID despite non-standard format: {output}\")\n712\t            return output\n713\t\n714\t        # Last resort: return the whole output (bf might have changed format)\n715\t        logger.warning(f\"Unexpected bf create output format: {output}\")\n716\t        return output\n717\t\n718\t    def build_pending_card(\n719\t        self,\n720\t        request: EscalateRequest,\n721\t        bead_id: str,\n722\t        bead_type: str = \"task\",\n723\t    ) -> dict:\n724\t        \"\"\"\n725\t        Build a pending-card spec for the surface.\n726\t\n727\t        The pending card shows the user that work is in progress\n728\t        and will be delivered when the bead closes.\n729\t        \"\"\"\n730\t        return {\n731\t            \"type\": \"pending\",\n732\t            \"id\": f\"pending-{bead_id}\",\n733\t            \"intent_id\": request.intent_id,\n734\t            \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n735\t            \"title\": self._generate_bead_title(request),\n736\t            \"summary\": f\"Working on: {request.utterance[:100]}\",\n737\t            \"status\": \"pending\",\n738\t            \"urgency\": request.metadata.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n739\t            \"created_at\": int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n740\t            \"metadata\": {\n741\t                \"project_slug\": request.project_slug,\n742\t                \"topic_id\": request.topic_id,\n743\t                \"bead_type\": bead_type,\n744\t            },\n745\t        }\n746\t\n747\t    async def escalate_intent(self, request: EscalateRequest) -> EscalateResult:\n748\t        \"\"\"\n749\t        Escalate an intent to a bead.\n750\t\n751\t        Full flow:\n752\t        1. Load exceptions.yaml to determine bead type from escalation_targets\n753\t        2. Evaluate auto-approve rules from exceptions.yaml\n754\t        3. If auto-approved: execute directly and return result\n755\t        4. Otherwise: formulate bead body via LLM, create bead, return pending-card spec\n756\t\n757\t        Args:\n758\t            request: The escalate request\n759\t\n760\t        Returns:\n761\t            EscalateResult with bead ID and pending card (if bead created)\n762\t            or execution result (if auto-approved)\n763\t        \"\"\"\n764\t        logger.info(f\"Escalating intent {request.intent_id}\")\n765\t\n766\t        try:\n767\t            # Step 1: Load exceptions.yaml\n768\t            reload_mgr = self._get_reload_manager()\n769\t            exceptions_config = reload_mgr.get_config(\"exceptions\")\n770\t\n771\t            # Step 2: Evaluate auto-approve rules\n772\t            auto_approve, reason = self._evaluate_auto_approve(request, exceptions_config)\n773\t\n774\t            if auto_approve:\n775\t                logger.info(f\"Auto-approved intent {request.intent_id}: {reason}\")\n776\t\n777\t                # Execute directly without bead creation\n778\t                execution_result = await self._execute_auto_approved(request)\n779\t\n780\t                # Update intent in store\n781\t                store = await self._get_store()\n782\t                await store.update_intent_status(\n783\t                    intent_id=request.intent_id,\n784\t                    status=\"resolved\",\n785\t                )\n786\t\n787\t                # Return result with empty pending_card (auto-approved, no bead created)\n788\t                return EscalateResult(\n789\t                    bead_id=\"\",  # No bead created\n790\t                    intent_id=request.intent_id,\n791\t                    pending_card=execution_result,  # Return execution result directly\n792\t                    status=\"completed\",\n793\t                )\n794\t\n795\t            # Step 3: Determine bead type from escalation_targets\n796\t            bead_type = self._get_bead_type_from_targets(request.intent_type, exceptions_config)\n797\t            logger.info(f\"Creating bead type '{bead_type}' for intent {request.intent_id} (not auto-approved: {reason})\")\n798\t\n799\t            # Step 4: Formulate bead body\n800\t            bead_body = await self.formulate_bead_body(request)\n801\t\n802\t            # Step 5: Create bead with determined type\n803\t            bead_id = await self._create_bead_with_type(request, bead_body, bead_type)\n804\t\n805\t            # Step 5.5: Create bead_watch row for circuit breaker tracking\n806\t            # (plan §10 The Async Path: watcher tracks open beads for refusals/SLA)\n807\t            await self._create_bead_watch(\n808\t                bead_ref=bead_id,\n809\t                project_slug=request.project_slug,\n810\t                intent_type=request.intent_type,\n811\t            )\n812\t\n813\t            # Step 6: Build pending card\n814\t            pending_card = self.build_pending_card(request, bead_id, bead_type)\n815\t\n816\t            # Update intent in store with bead reference\n817\t            store = await self._get_store()\n818\t            await
