The watch task is cancelled\n190\t        explicitly as well: cancelling a supervisor awaiting another task does\n191\t        not automatically cancel the awaited task.\n192\t        \"\"\"\n193\t        self._running = False\n194\t        supervisor = self._supervisor_task\n195\t        if supervisor and not supervisor.done():\n196\t            supervisor.cancel()\n197\t            try:\n198\t                await supervisor\n199\t            except asyncio.CancelledError:\n200\t                pass\n201\t        watch = self._watch_task\n202\t        if watch and not watch.done():\n203\t            watch.cancel()\n204\t            try:\n205\t                await watch\n206\t            except BaseException:  # noqa: BLE001 — tearing down, swallow anything\n207\t                pass\n208\t        logger.info(\"Bead watcher stopped\")\n209\t\n210\t    async def _supervise(self) -> None:\n211\t        \"\"\"Supervise the watch task: restart it on crash with backoff.\n212\t\n213\t        The watch loop catches per-iteration (transient) exceptions itself and\n214\t        keeps polling — those never reach here. This layer handles the case the\n215\t        inner loop cannot: the task ending outright (an exception that is not a\n216\t        transient ``Exception`` -- e.g. a ``BaseException`` raised mid-tick --\n217\t        or the loop returning). On such a death, while we are still meant to be\n218\t        running, we log and respawn the task after an exponentially-growing\n219\t        backoff (2s, 4s, 8s, ... capped at 32s). Backoff resets to the short\n220\t        end once a tick completes successfully (see ``_watch_loop``).\n221\t\n222\t        ``stop()`` sets ``_running = False`` before cancelling the supervisor,\n223\t        so a clean shutdown is distinguished from an unexpected task death by\n224\t        the ``_running`` flag rather than by exception type.\n225\t        \"\"\"\n226\t        while self._running:\n227\t            self._watch_task = asyncio.create_task(\n228\t                self._watch_loop(),\n229\t                name=f\"bead-watcher-tick-{self._restart_count}\",\n230\t            )\n231\t            crash_reason: Optional[BaseException] = None\n232\t            try:\n233\t                await self._watch_task\n234\t            except asyncio.CancelledError:\n235\t                # Supervisor itself cancelled -- only stop() does that. Exit.\n236\t                return\n237\t            except (KeyboardInterrupt, SystemExit):\n238\t                # Process-level signals: never swallow, let them propagate.\n239\t                raise\n240\t            except BaseException as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — must catch task death\n241\t                crash_reason = exc\n242\t\n243\t            if not self._running:\n244\t                # stop() raced with the task ending -- clean shutdown.\n245\t                return\n246\t\n247\t            self._restart_count += 1\n248\t            backoff = self._next_backoff()\n249\t            if crash_reason is not None:\n250\t                logger.warning(\n251\t                    \"Bead watcher task crashed (%s); restarting in %.1fs \"\n252\t                    \"(restart #%d)\",\n253\t                    type(crash_reason).__name__,\n254\t                    backoff,\n255\t                    self._restart_count,\n256\t                    exc_info=True,\n257\t                )\n258\t            else:\n259\t                logger.warning(\n260\t                    \"Bead watcher task ended unexpectedly; restarting in %.1fs \"\n261\t                    \"(restart #%d)\",\n262\t                    backoff,\n263\t                    self._restart_count,\n264\t                )\n265\t            try:\n266\t                await asyncio.sleep(backoff)\n267\t            except asyncio.CancelledError:\n268\t                return\n269\t\n270\t    def _next_backoff(self) -> float:\n271\t        \"\"\"Backoff (seconds) for the upcoming restart, growing with crashes.\n272\t\n273\t        ``_restart_count`` is incremented to >= 1 before each call, so the\n274\t        first restart waits RESTART_BACKOFF_SECONDS[0] (2s), the next the\n275\t        following entry (4s), and so on; once the count exceeds the schedule\n276\t        the final (cap) value is reused.\n277\t        \"\"\"\n278\t        idx = min(self._restart_count - 1, len(self.RESTART_BACKOFF_SECONDS) - 1)\n279\t        return self.RESTART_BACKOFF_SECONDS[idx]\n280\t\n281\t    def _stamp_tick(self) -> None:\n282\t        \"\"\"Record liveness after a completed poll tick.\n283\t\n284\t        Called after every tick body resolves (success or a caught transient\n285\t        error). GET /health's watcher block reads these: ``last_tick_at``\n286\t        within 2x the interval means the task is alive, and ``tick_count`` is\n287\t        the cumulative tick count. (adc-4afi, consumed by child 3.)\n288\t        \"\"\"\n289\t        self.last_tick_at = time.time()\n290\t        self.tick_count += 1\n291\t\n292\t    def health_snapshot(self) -> dict:\n293\t        \"\"\"Return watcher liveness snapshot for GET /health.\n294\t\n295\t        Computes ``alive`` as: the watch task is running AND the last tick\n296\t        occurred within 2x the poll interval. Returns a dict with ``alive``,\n297\t        ``last_tick_at`` (epoch seconds or None if never ticked), ``tick_count``,\n298\t        and ``interval`` (seconds). If ``last_tick_at`` is 0.0 (never ticked),\n299\t        ``last_tick_at`` is None and ``alive`` is False.\n300\t\n301\t        Also includes monitoring tick stats (plan §10: Ambient monitoring tick).\n302\t        \"\"\"\n303\t        now = time.time()\n304\t        # Task is running if the supervisor task exists and is not done/cancelled\n305\t        task_running = (\n306\t            self._watch_task is not None\n307\t            and not self._watch_task.done()\n308\t        )\n309\t        # Last tick is fresh if within 2x the interval (0.0 means never ticked)\n310\t        tick_fresh = (\n311\t            self.last_tick_at > 0.0\n312\t            and (now - self.last_tick_at) <= (2 * self.check_interval_seconds)\n313\t        )\n314\t        return {\n315\t            \"alive\": task_running and tick_fresh,\n316\t            \"last_tick_at\": int(self.last_tick_at) if self.last_tick_at > 0.0 else None,\n317\t            \"tick_count\": self.tick_count,\n318\t            \"interval\": int(self.check_interval_seconds),\n319\t            \"monitoring\": {\n320\t                \"last_tick_at\": int(self.last_monitoring_tick_at) if self.last_monitoring_tick_at > 0.0 else None,\n321\t                \"tick_count\": self.monitoring_tick_count,\n322\t                \"interval\": int(self._monitoring_tick_interval),\n323\t                \"config_mtime\": self._monitoring_config_mtime,\n324\t            },\n325\t        }\n326\t\n327\t    async def _watch_loop(self) -> None:\n328\t        \"\"\"Main watch loop - checks for new bead events each interval.\n329\t\n330\t        Each iteration runs the detection tick (``_check_for_events``), stamps\n331\t        liveness, then sleeps for the poll interval. Per-iteration exceptions\n332\t        are caught and logged so a transient error never ends the task -- the\n333\t        supervisor layer restarts the task only when it truly dies. A completed\n334\t        tick (success or caught transient error) resets the crash backoff.\n335\t        \"\"\"\n336\t        while self._running:\n337\t            try:\n338\t                await self._check_for_events()\n339\t            except asyncio.CancelledError:\n340\t                raise  # shutdown signal -- let the supervisor see it\n341\t            except Exception as e:\n342\t                logger.error(f\"Error in bead watch loop: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n343\t            # Stamp liveness after the tick body resolves. A completed tick --\n344\t            # success or a caught transient error -- means the task is healthy,\n345\t            # so reset the crash backoff schedule.\n346\t            self._stamp_tick()\n347\t            self._restart_count = 0\n348\t            try:\n349\t                await asyncio.sleep(self.check_interval_seconds)\n350\t            except asyncio.CancelledError:\n351\t                raise\n352\t\n353\t    async def _check_for_events(self) -> None:\n354\t        \"\"\"Poll for newly-closed bead events and route each to surfaces.\n355\t\n356\t        Detection is CLI-only (plan §10): each tick runs `bf list --status\n357\t        closed` and emits only closures newer than the close-timestamp\n358\t        high-water mark. Every emitted record is handed to the next stage\n359\t        (``_process_bead_event``); this layer does not resolve intents or write\n360\t        results itself (child 4 owns the close -> result path).\n361\t\n362\t        Also runs circuit breaker check (plan §10 The Async Path):\n363\t        polls open tracked beads for REFUSED comments, updates bead_watch state,\n364\t        fences beads that meet criteria, and creates stuck cards.\n365\t\n366\t        Also runs ambient monitoring tick (plan §10 Ambient monitoring tick):\n367\t        hot-reloads config and evaluates monitoring rules.\n368\t        \"\"\"\n369\t        events = await self._poll_closed_beads()\n370\t        for event in events:\n371\t            await self._process_bead_event(event)\n372\t\n373\t        # Circuit breaker tick (plan §10 The Async Path)\n374\t        await self._check_circuit_breaker()\n375\t\n376\t        # Ambient monitoring tick (plan §10 Ambient monitoring tick)\n377\t        await self._ambient_monitoring_tick()\n378\t\n379\t    async def _poll_closed_beads(self) -> list[BeadEvent]:\n380\t        \"\"\"Poll closed beads via the CLI and return the newly-closed subset.\n381\t\n382\t        Maintains ``self._close_highwater`` (newest close timestamp already\n383\t        processed, UTC epoch seconds). Each tick returns only beads closed\n384\t        strictly AFTER that mark and advances the mark to the newest close\n385\t        time seen this tick.\n386\t\n387\t        First poll (and first after any restart, since the mark is in-memory):\n388\t        seed the mark to the newest existing close time and emit nothing,\n389\t        instead of delivering the entire already-closed backlog. This is the\n390\t        documented high-water-mark semantics -- a restart re-reads but does\n391\t        not re-deliver already-closed beads; only closures after the mark\n392\t        surface. (adc-qw85)\n393\t        \"\"\"\n394\t        records = await self._run_bf_list_closed()\n395\t\n396\t        # (close_epoch, record) for records whose close time parsed; sorted\n397\t        # ascending so the last entry is the newest close this tick.\n398\t        parsed: list[tuple[float, dict]] = []\n399\t        for rec in records:\n400\t            ts = self._parse_close_epoch(rec.get(\"closed_at\"))\n401\t            if ts is None:\n402\t                continue\n403\t            parsed.append((ts, rec))\n404\t        parsed.sort(key=lambda pair: pair[0])\n405\t\n406\t        if self._close_highwater is None:\n407\t            # Baseline against the current backlog without delivering it.\n408\t            if parsed:\n409\t                self._close_highwater = parsed[-1][0]\n410\t                logger.debug(\n411\t                    \"Bead watcher baseline: high-water mark set to %.6f \"\n412\t                    \"(%d closed beads already seen; none re-delivered).\",\n413\t                    self._close_highwater, len(parsed),\n414\t                )\n415\t            return []\n416\t\n417\t        # Emit only closures strictly newer than the mark.\n418\t        new_events: list[BeadEvent] = []\n419\t        for ts, rec in parsed:\n420\t            if ts <= self._close_highwater:\n421\t                continue\n422\t            new_events.append(BeadEvent(\n423\t                bead_id=rec.get(\"id\", \"\"),\n424\t                event_type=\"closed\",\n425\t                timestamp=int(ts),\n426\t                data=rec,\n427\t            ))\n428\t        if new_events:\n429\t            # Advance to the newest close seen overall this tick (parsed is\n430\t            # sorted ascending, so the last is the max). Every record newer\n431\t            # than the old mark was emitted, so this is also the newest emitted.\n432\t            self._close_highwater = parsed[-1][0]\n433\t        return new_events\n434\t\n435\t    async def _check_circuit_breaker(self) -> None:\n436\t        \"\"\"Check circuit breaker conditions for all open watched beads.\n437\t\n438\t        Plan §10 The Async Path:\n439\t        - Poll open tracked beads (every unresolved intents.bead_ref) via bf show\n440\t        - Parse REFUSED: comments past the high-water mark\n441\t        - Persist refusal counts and reasons in bead_watch table\n442\t        - Fence beads meeting criteria (3 refusals OR 24h age)\n443\t        - Set intent status to 'stuck'\n444\t        - Push 'task stuck — needs your input' card to active surface\n445\t        \"\"\"\n446\t        try:\n447\t            # Step 1: Get all open watched beads\n448\t            watched_beads = await self.store.get_open_watched_beads()\n449\t\n450\t            if not watched_beads:\n451\t                logger.debug(\"No open watched beads for circuit breaker check\")\n452\t                return\n453\t\n454\t            logger.debug(f\"Circuit breaker checking {len(watched_beads)} open beads\")\n455\t\n456\t            # Step 2: For each bead, fetch comments and parse refusals\n457\t            for watch_row in watched_beads:\n458\t                bead_ref = watch_row[\"bead_ref\"]\n459\t                high_water = watch_row[\"comment_high_water\"]\n460\t\n461\t                # Fetch bead details via bf show\n462\t                bead_details = await self._run_bf_show(bead_ref)\n463\t\n464\t                if not bead_details:\n465\t                    logger.warning(f\"Could not fetch bead {bead_ref} for circuit breaker check\")\n466\t                    continue\n467\t\n468\t                # Parse comments for refusals\n469\t                refusals = self._parse_refusals_from_comments(\n470\t                    bead_details.get(\"comments\", []),\n471\t                    since_index=high_water,\n472\t                )\n473\t\n474\t                # Update bead_watch state if we found new refusals\n475\t                if refusals:\n476\t                    # Count ALL refusals found, not just the latest one\n477\t                    refusal_count = len(refusals)\n478\t                    latest_refusal = refusals[-1]  # Most recent\n479\t                    latest_index = latest_refusal[\"index\"]\n480\t                    latest_reason = latest_refusal[\"reason\"]\n481\t\n482\t                    # Update refusal count by the number of refusals found\n483\t                    # (not just 1, since we may have multiple new refusals in one tick)\n484\t                    await self.store.update_bead_watch_refusal(\n485\t                        bead_ref=bead_ref,\n486\t                        refusal_reason=latest_reason,\n487\t                        comment_index=latest_index,\n488\t                        refusal_count_add=refusal_count,\n489\t                    )\n490\t\n491\t                    logger.info(\n492\t                        f\"Bead {bead_ref}: recorded {refusal_count} refusals \"\n493\t                        f\"(reason: {latest_reason[:50]}...)\"\n494\t                    )\n495\t\n496\t            # Step 3: Check SLA flags and flag beads past deadline\n497\t            await self._check_and_flag_sla_beads()\n498\t\n499\t            # Step 4: Fence beads that meet criteria\n500\t            await self._fence_needs_fencing_beads()\n501\t\n502\t        except Exception as e:\n503\t            logger.error(f\"Error in circuit breaker check: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n504\t\n505\t    async def _check_and_flag_sla_beads(self) -> None:\n506\t        \"\"\"Flag beads that have passed their SLA deadline.\n507\t\n508\t        Plan §10 The Async Path: Visible Aging - cards past SLA are flagged.\n509\t        \"\"\"\n510\t        try:\n511\t            past_sla = await self.store.get_beads_past_sla()\n512\t\n513\t            for bead_watch in past_sla:\n514\t                bead_ref = bead_watch[\"bead_ref\"]\n515\t                await self.store.flag_sla(bead_ref)\n516\t                logger.info(f\"Flagged SLA for bead {bead_ref} (past deadline)\")\n517\t\n518\t        except Exception as e:\n519\t            logger.error(f\"Error checking SLA deadlines: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n520\t\n521\t    async def _fence_needs_fencing_beads(self) -> None:\n522\t        \"\"\"Fence beads that meet circuit breaker criteria.\n523\t\n524\t        Plan §10 The Async Path: after N refusals (default 3) or T hours without\n525\t        progress (default 24h), fence the bead to status=blocked, set intent status\n526\t        to 'stuck', and push a 'task stuck — needs your input' card.\n527\t        \"\"\"\n528\t        try:\n529\t            needs_fencing = await self.store.get_beads_needing_fencing()\n530\t\n531\t            for bead_watch in needs_fencing:\n532\t                bead_ref = {e}\", exc_info=True)\n540\t\n541\t    async def _fence_bead(self, bead_ref: str, refusal_reason: str, refusal_count: int) -> None:\n542\t        \"\"\"Fence a single bead: block it, mark intent stuck, create stuck card.\n543\t\n544\t        Args:\n545\t            bead_ref: The bead ID to fence\n546\t            refusal_reason: The reason for fencing (most recent refusal or age)\n547\t            refusal_count: Number of refusals recorded\n548\t        \"\"\"\n549\t        logger.info(f\"Fencing bead {bead_ref} (refusals: {refusal_count})\")\n550\t\n551\t        # Step 1: Run bf update --status blocked\n552\t        try:\n553\t            await self._run_bf_update_status(bead_ref, \"blocked\")\n554\t            logger.info(f\"Set bead {bead_ref} to blocked status\")\n555\t        except Exception as e:\n556\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to block bead {bead_ref}: {e}\")\n557\t            # Continue to intent update even if bf fails\n558\t\n559\t        # Step 2: Mark fenced in bead_watch\n560\t        await self.store.fence_bead(bead_ref)\n561\t\n562\t        # Step 3: Find intent and set status to 'stuck'\n563\t        intent = await self.store.get_intent_by_bead_ref(bead_ref)\n564\t\n565\t        if not intent:\n566\t            logger.warning(f\"No intent found for bead {bead_ref}; cannot set stuck status\")\n567\t           logger.warning(f\"Intent {intent_id} has no topic_id; cannot create stuck card\")\n575\t            return\n576\t\n577\t        # Step 4: Update intent status to 'stuck'\n578\t        await     broadcaster = get_broadcaster()\n583\t        await broadcaster.broadcast(\n584\t            SSEEvent(\n585\t                event_type=EventType.TASK_STUCK,\n586\t                data={\n587\t                    \"bead_id\": bead_ref,\n588\t                    \"stuck_reason\": refusal_reason,\n589\t                    \"refusal_count\": refusal_count,\n590\t                    \"intent_id\":           },\n595\t                target_session_id=session_id,\n596\t            )\n597\t        )\n598\t\n599\t        # Step 6: Create stuck result card\n600\t        await refusal_reason=refusal_reason,\n606\t            refusal_count=refusal_count,\n607\t        )\n608\t\n609\t    async def _create_stuck_card(\n610\t        self,\n611\t        bead_ref: str,\n612\t        intent_id: str,\n613\t        session_id: str,\n614\t        topic_id: str,\n615\t        refusal_reason: str,\n616\t        refusal_count: int,\n617\t    ) -> None:\n618\t        \"\"\"Create a — needs your input\"\n623\t\n624\t        # Build card data\n625\t        data = {\n626\t            \"bead_id\": bead_ref,\n627\t            \"stuck_reason\": refusal_reason,\n628\t intent_id=intent_id,\n636\t            topic_id=topic_id,\n637\t            session_id=session_id,\n638\t            summary=summary,\n639\t            data=data,\n640\t            urgency=\"high\",  # Stuck tasks are high urgency\n641\t        )\n642\t\n643\t        logger.info(f\"Created stuck card {result_id} for bead {bead_ref}\")\n644\t\n645\t        # Broadcast result to active surfaces\n646\t        result_for_broadcast = {\n647\t            \"id\": result_id,\n648\t            \"result_id\": result_id,\n649\t            \"intent_id\": intent_id,\n650\t            \"topic_id\": topic_id,\n651\t            \"session_id\": session_id,\n652\t            \"summary\": summary,\n653\t            \"data\": data,\n654\t            \"urgency\": \"high\",\n655\t            \"created_at\": int(datetime.now().timestamp()),\n656\t        }\n657\t\n658\t        decision = await self.router.route_result(\n659\t            session_id=session_id,\n660\t            origin_surface_id=None,\n661\t            urgency=\"high\",\n662\t        )\n663\t\n664\t        if decision.target_surfaces:\n665\t            for surface in decision.target_surfaces:\n666\t broadcast_result(\n670\t                        result=result_for_broadcast,\n671\t                        session_id=session_id,\n672\t                        target_surface_id=surface.id,\n673\t                    )\n674\t        elif decision.fallback_used:\n675\t            await self._send_to_telegram(result_for_broadcast, session_id)\n676\t        else:\n677\t            logger.info(f\"No surface available for stuck card of bead {bead_ref}\")\n678\t\n679\t    async def _run_bf_show(self, bead_ref: str) -> Optional[dict]:\n680\t        \"\"\"Run `bf show <bead_ref>` and return parsed    await proc.wait()\n704\t            logger.warning(f\"bf show {bead_ref} timed out\")\n705\t            return None\n706\t\n707\t        if proc.returncode != 0:\n708\t    return None\n713\t\n714\t        try:\n715\t            bead_data = json.loads(stdout_b.decode(errors=\"replace\").strip())\n716\t            return bead_data\n717\t        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n718\t            logger.warning(f\"Failed to parse bf show output for {bead_ref}: {e}\")\n719\t            return None\n720\t\n721\t    async def _run_bf_update_status(self, bead_ref: str, status: str) -> None:\n722\t        \"\"\"Run `bf update --status <status> <bead_ref>` to fence a bead.\n723\t\n724\t        Raises an exception if the command fails (non-zero exit, timeout, etc.).\n725\t        \"\"\"\n726\t        try:\n727\t            proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n728\t                self._bf_bin,\n729\t                \"update\", \"--status\", status, bead_ref,\n730\t                cwd=self._bf_workspace,\n731\t                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n732\t                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n733\t            )\n734\t        except (FileNotFoundError, OSError) as e:\n735\t            raise RuntimeError(f\"Failed to spawn bf update: {e}\") from e\n736\t\n737\t        try:\n738\t            stdout_b, stderr_b = await asyncio.wait_for(\n739\t                proc.communicate(), timeout=self._subprocess_timeout\n740\t            )\n741\t        except asyncio.TimeoutError:\n742\t            proc.kill()\n743\t            await proc.wait()\n744\t            raise RuntimeError(f\"bf update {bead_ref} timed out\")\n745\t\n746\t        if {proc.returncode}: {error_msg[:200]}\"\n750\t            )\n751\t\n752\t    @staticmethod\n753\t    def _parse_refusals_from_comments(\n754\t        comments: list, since_index: int\n755\t    ) -> list[dict]:\n756\t        \"\"\"Parse REFUSED: comments from bead comment list.\n757\t\n758\t        Args:\n759\t            comments: List of comment dicts from bf show\n760\t            since_index: Only parse comments with index > this\n761\t\n762\t        Returns:\n763\t            List of {index, reason, created_at} for each REFUSED: comment found\n764\t        \"\"\"\n765\t        REFUSED_PATTERN = re.compile(r\"^\\s*REFUSED:\\s*(.+)$\", re.MULTILINE)\n766\t\n767\t        refusals: list[dict] = []\n768\t\n769\t        for idx, comment in enumerate(comments):\n770\t            # Skip comments at or before the high-water mark\n771\t            # (only process comments with index > since_index)\n772\t            if idx <= since_index:\n773\t                continue\n774\t\n775\t            comment_body = comment.get(\"body\", \"\")\n776\t            if not isinstance(comment_body, str):\n777\t                continue\n778\t\n779\t            # Check for REFUSED: prefix\n780\t            match = REFUSED_PATTERN.search(comment_body)\n781\t            if match:\n782\t                reason = match.group(1).strip()\n783\t                refusals.append({\n784\t                    \"index\": idx,\n785\t                    \"reason\": reason,\n786\t                    \"created_at\": comment.get(\"created_at\"),\n787\t                })\n788\t\n789\t        return refusals\n790\t\n791\t    @staticmethod\n792\t    def _parse_close_epoch(closed_at: object) -> Optional[float]:\n793\t        \"\"\"Parse a bf ``closed_at`` (RFC3339, up to nanosecond) to UTC epoch.\n794\t\n795\t        bf emits close times with nanosecond precision and a trailing ``Z`` --\n796\t        e.g. ``2026-07-22T12:47:22.595899004Z`` -- though some records omit the\n797\t        fractional part entirely. ``datetime.fromisoformat`` (3.11+) accepts\n798\t        both forms and yields a UTC-aware datetime, so ``.timestamp()`` is\n799\t        correct regardless of the host's local timezone. Returns None on any\n800\t        parse failure (a malformed/missing close time is logged, not fatal).\n801\t        \"\"\"\n802\t        if not isinstance(closed_at, str) or not closed_at.strip():\n803\t            return None\n804\t        try:\n805\t            return datetime.fromisoformat(closed_at.strip()).timestamp()\n806\t        except (ValueError, TypeError) as e:\n807\t            logger.warning(\"Could not parse closed_at %r: %s\", closed_at, e)\n808\t            return None\n809\t\n810\t    async def _run_bf_list_closed(self) -> list[dict]:\n811\t        \"\"\"Run `bf list --status closed --json` and return parsed bead records.\n812\t\n813\t        The CLI is the sole source of bead state (plan §10: never read the bf\n814\t        workspace's private store files directly). bf runs from the\n815\t        aide-de-camp checkout (the beads workspace; Beads-Workspace Scoping) so\n816\t        it sees every bead this app owns.\n817\t\n818\t        Output is JSONL -- one JSON object per line; each non-blank line is\n819\t        parsed independently so one malformed line does not discard the rest.\n820\t        Every failure mode (missing binary, spawn error, timeout, non-zero\n821\t        exit, unparseable line) is caught and logged, returning an empty list:\n822\t        one tick's failure must not kill the watch task -- the lifespan\n823\t        supervisor (child 1) is the backstop. A caught failure simply means\n824\t        \"no new closures detected this tick.\" (adc-qw85)\n825\t        \"\"\"\n826\t        try:\n827\t            proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n828\t                self._bf_bin,\n829\t                \"list\", \"--status\", \"closed\", \"--json\",\n830\t                cwd=self._bf_workspace,\n831\t                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n832\t                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n833\t            )\n834\t        except FileNotFoundError:\n835\t            logger.error(\n836\t                \"bf binary %r not found -- bead-close detection skipped this \"\n837\t                \"tick. Ensure bead-forge (bf) is installed and on PATH.\",\n838\t                self._bf_bin,\n839\t            )\n840\t            return []\n841\t        except OSError as e:\n842\t            logger.error(\"Failed to spawn bf list: %s\", e, exc_info=True)\n843\t            return []\n844\t\n845\t        try:\n846\t            stdout_b, stderr_b = await asyncio.wait_for(\n847\t                proc.communicate(), timeout=self._subprocess_timeout\n848\t            )\n849\t        except asyncio.TimeoutError:\n850\t            proc.kill()\n851\t            await proc.wait()\n852\t            logger.warning(\n853\t                \"bf list --status closed timed out after %.1fs\",\n854\t                self._subprocess_timeout,\n855\t            )\n856\t            return []\n857\t\n858\t        if proc.returncode != 0:\n859\t            logger.warning(\n860\t                \"bf list --status closed exited %s: %s\",\n861\t                proc.returncode,\n862\t                stderr_b.decode(errors=\"replace\").strip()[:500],\n863\t            )\n864\t            return []\n865\t\n866\t        records: list[dict] = []\n867\t        for lineno, line in enumerate(\n868\t            stdout_b.decode(errors=\"replace\").splitlines(), start=1\n869\t        ):\n870\t            line = line.strip()\n871\t            if not line:\n872\t                continue\n873\t            try:\n874\t                records.append(json.loads(line))\n875\t            except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n876\t                logger.warning(\n877\t                    \"Skipping unparseable bf list line %d: %s\", lineno, e\n878\t                )\n879\t        return records\n880\t\n881\t    def _extract_metadata(self, bead: dict) -> dict:\n882\t        \"\"\"\n883\t        Reconstruct routing metadata from a bead's flat `labels` array.\n884\t\n885\t        escalate/handler.py's `_create_bead` / `_create_bead_with_type` encode\n886\t        session_id, origin_surface_id, urgency, etc. as `key=value` label\n887\t        strings (e.g. `session_id=session-1`). bf issues have NO nested\n888\t        `metadata` object -- only a flat `labels` list -- so we parse those\n889\t        `key=value` entries back into a dict, matching the encoding the escalate\n890\t        handler writes. Labels without `=` (e.g. `deferred`, `split-child`) and\n891\t        `:`-style labels (e.g. `failure-count:1`) are ignored.\n892\t        \"\"\"\n893\t        metadata: dict = {}\n894\t        labels = bead.get(\"labels\", [])\n895\t        if not isinstance(labels, list):\n896\t            return metadata\n897\t        for label in labels:\n898\t            if not isinstance(label, str) or \"=\" not in label:\n899\t                continue\n900\t            key, _, value = label.partition(\"=\")\n901\t            key = key.strip()\n902\t            if key:\n903\t                metadata[key] = value.strip()\n904\t        return metadata\n905\t\n906\t    async def _process_bead_event(self, event: BeadEvent) -> None:\n907\t        \"\"\"Process a closed bead event: resolve intent, write result, route to surfaces.\n908\t\n909\t        For each closed bead:\n910\t        1. Look up intent via intents.bead_ref (not label parsing)\n911\t        2. If no matching intent, skip (most bf beads are not escalate-tracked)\n912\t        3. Write results row via store.create_result\n913\t        4. Mark intent resolved\n914\t        5. SSE push using existing router (with Telegram fallback)\n915\t        \"\"\"\n916\t        bead_id = event.bead_id\n917\t        bead_data = event.data\n918\t\n919\t        # Step 1: Look up intent by bead_ref (NOT from labels)\n920\t        intent = await self.store.get_intent_by_bead_ref(bead_id)\n921\t\n922\t        if not intent:\n923\t            # No matching intent - not an escalate-tracked bead.\n924\t            # This is expected for most bf beads, so debug-log only.\n925\t            logger.debug(f\"Closed bead {bead_id} has no matching intent.bead_ref; skipping\")\n926\t            return\n927\t\n928\t        # Extract intent context\n929\t        intent_id = intent[\"id\"]\n930\t        session_id = intent[\"session_id\"]\n931\t        topic_id = intent.get(\"topic_id\")\n932\t\n933\t        if not topic_id:\n934\t            logger.warning(f\"Intent {intent_id} for bead {bead_id} has no topic_id; skipping result write\")\n935\t            return\n936\t\n937\t        # Step 2: Extract result from bead (reusing existing body logic)\n938\t        result = await self._extract_result_from_bead(bead_data, session_id)\n939\t\n940\t        if not result:\n941\t            logger.warning(f\"Could not extract result from bead {bead_id}\")\n942\t            return\n943\t\n944\t        # Step 3: Write results row via store.create_result\n945\t        # Build result data matching the create_result signature\n946\t        summary = result.get(\"summary\", \"Bead resolved\")\n947\t        data = result.get(\"data\", {})\n948\t        urgency = result.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\")\n949\t\n950\t        try:\n951\t            result_id = await self.store.create_result(\n952\t                intent_id=intent_id,\n953\t                topic_id=topic_id,\n954\t                session_id=session_id,\n955\t                summary=summary,\n956\t                data=data,\n957\t                urgency=urgency,\n958\t            )\n959\t            logger.debug(f\"Created result {result_id} for bead {bead_id}\")\n960\t        except Exception as e:\n961\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to create result for bead {bead_id}: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n962\t            return\n963\t\n964\t        # Construct the result dict for SSE broadcast (must include result_id)\n965\t        result_for_broadcast = {\n966\t            \"id\": result_id,\n967\t            \"result_id\": result_id,  # For test compatibility\n968\t            \"intent_id\": intent_id,\n969\t            \"topic_id\": topic_id,\n970\t            \"session_id\": session_id,\n971\t            \"summary\": summary,\n972\t            \"data\": data,\n973\t            \"urgency\": urgency,\n974\t            \"created_at\": int(datetime.now().timestamp()),\n975\t        }\n976\t\n977\t        # Step 4: Mark intent resolved\n978\t        try:\n979\t            await self.store.update_intent_status(intent_id, \"resolved\")\n980\t            logger.debug(f\"Marked intent {intent_id} resolved for bead {bead_id}\")\n981\t        except Exception as e:\n982\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to mark intent {intent_id} resolved: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n983\t            # Continue to routing even if status update fails\n984\t\n985\t        # Step 5: SSE push per Surface Routing Rules\n986\t        # Use existing router + broadcast_result / telegram fallback path\n987\t        # Get routing decision - no origin_surface_id from bead, use None\n988\t        decision = await self.router.route_result(\n989\t            session_id=session_id,\n990\t            origin_surface_id=None,\n991\t            urgency=urgency,\n992\t        )\n993\t\n994\t        # Route to target surfaces\n995\t        if decision.target_surfaces:\n996\t            for surface in decision.target_surfaces:\n997\t                if surface.type == \"telegram\":\n998\t                    await self._send_to_telegram(result_for_broadcast, session_id)\n999\t                else:\n1000\t                    #
